scRNA Seurat Analysis

2026-03-26

Important commands in scRNA Seurat analysis

Read10X()
CreateSeuratObject() # for each replicate
PercentageFeatureSet() # to add % mitochondrial gene
subset() # filter outliner for each replicate
list() # combine control and disease data into a list
NormalizeData() # inside lapply(), for each dataset independently 
FindVariableFeatures() # inside lapply(), for each dataset independently 
SelectIntegrationFeatures() # select features that are repeatedly variable across replicates
FindIntegrationAnchors() # correct technical difference among replicates
IntegrateData()
DefaultAssay() <- 'integrated'

ScaleData()       
RunPCA() # stored in CONIPF.combined@reduction$pca
ElbowPlot() # to see how many PCs to include in the next steps
FindNeighbors()
FindClusters()
RunUMAP() # stored in CONIPF.combined@reductions$umap
RunTSNE() # stored in CONIPF.combined@reductions$tsne, Safe to run sequentially after RunUMAP

DimPlot() # UMAP plot

DotPlot() # show gene expression across clusters
RenameIdents() # rename clusters
FeaturePlot() # show gene's location in what cluster
VlnPlot()
DoHeatmap()

Load Libraries

# Install packages from CRAN
install.packages(c("Seurat", "tidyverse", "patchwork"))
library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)

Read barcodes, features (genes), matrix files

Follow this video and this video to download GSE132771 data from GEO Accession Viewer, then format them correctly before reading them into R.

There’re 3 Control (NML) replicates and 3 Disease (IPF) replicates to be downloaded.

CON_1 <- Read10X(data.dir = "GSE132771_RAW/NML1")
CON_2 <- Read10X(data.dir = "GSE132771_RAW/NML2")
CON_3 <- Read10X(data.dir = "GSE132771_RAW/NML3")

IPF_1 <- Read10X(data.dir = "GSE132771_RAW/IPF1")
IPF_2 <- Read10X(data.dir = "GSE132771_RAW/IPF2")
IPF_3 <- Read10X(data.dir = "GSE132771_RAW/IPF3")

Create Seurat object and add percent mitochondrial genes

CON_1 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = CON_1, project = "CON", min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
CON_1 <- PercentageFeatureSet(CON_1, pattern = "^MT-", col.name = "percent.mt")

CON_2 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = CON_2, project = "CON", min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
CON_2 <- PercentageFeatureSet(CON_2, pattern = "^MT-", col.name = "percent.mt")

CON_3 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = CON_3, project = "CON", min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
CON_3 <- PercentageFeatureSet(CON_3, pattern = "^MT-", col.name = "percent.mt")


IPF_1 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = IPF_1, project = "IPF", min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
IPF_1 <- PercentageFeatureSet(IPF_1, pattern = "^MT-", col.name = "percent.mt")

IPF_2 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = IPF_2, project = "IPF", min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
IPF_2 <- PercentageFeatureSet(IPF_2, pattern = "^MT-", col.name = "percent.mt")

IPF_3 <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = IPF_3, project = "IPF", min.cells = 3, min.features = 200)
IPF_3 <- PercentageFeatureSet(IPF_3, pattern = "^MT-", col.name = "percent.mt")

Visualize data distribution to filter low quality cells

VlnPlot(CON_1, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)

CON_1 <- subset(CON_1, subset = nFeature_RNA < 4000 & nCount_RNA < 20000 & percent.mt < 10)
VlnPlot(CON_2, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)

CON_2 <- subset(CON_2, subset = nFeature_RNA < 4000 & nCount_RNA < 10000 & percent.mt < 10)
VlnPlot(CON_3, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)

CON_3 <- subset(CON_3, subset = nFeature_RNA < 4000 & nCount_RNA < 20000 & percent.mt < 10)
VlnPlot(IPF_1, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)

IPF_1 <- subset(IPF_1, subset = nFeature_RNA < 6000 & nCount_RNA < 30000 & percent.mt < 10)
VlnPlot(IPF_2, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)

IPF_2 <- subset(IPF_2, subset = nFeature_RNA < 3000 & nCount_RNA < 15000 & percent.mt < 5)
VlnPlot(IPF_3, features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"), ncol = 3)

IPF_3 <- subset(IPF_3, subset = nFeature_RNA < 4000 & nCount_RNA < 20000 & percent.mt < 10)

Combine control and disease data into a list

CONIPF.list <- list(CON_1 = CON_1, CON_2 = CON_2, CON_3 = CON_3,
                    IPF_1 = IPF_1, IPF_2 = IPF_2, IPF_3 = IPF_3)

Normalize and identify variable features for each dataset

CONIPF.list <- lapply(X = CONIPF.list, FUN = function(x) {
  x <- NormalizeData(x)
  x <- FindVariableFeatures(x, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000)
})

Select features that are repeatedly variable across datasets for integration

features <- SelectIntegrationFeatures(object.list = CONIPF.list)
CONIPF.anchors <- FindIntegrationAnchors(object.list = CONIPF.list, anchor.features = features)

Creates an ‘integrated’ data assay

CONIPF.combined <- IntegrateData(anchorset = CONIPF.anchors)

DefaultAssay(CONIPF.combined) <- "integrated"

Run the standard workflow for visualization and clustering

CONIPF.combined <- ScaleData(CONIPF.combined, verbose = FALSE)
CONIPF.combined <- RunPCA(CONIPF.combined, npcs = 50, verbose = FALSE)
ElbowPlot(CONIPF.combined, ndims = 50, reduction = 'pca')

CONIPF.combined <- RunUMAP(CONIPF.combined, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30)
CONIPF.combined <- FindNeighbors(CONIPF.combined, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30)
CONIPF.combined <- FindClusters(CONIPF.combined, resolution = 0.1)
## Modularity Optimizer version 1.3.0 by Ludo Waltman and Nees Jan van Eck
## 
## Number of nodes: 24314
## Number of edges: 1049396
## 
## Running Louvain algorithm...
## Maximum modularity in 10 random starts: 0.9803
## Number of communities: 15
## Elapsed time: 4 seconds
DimPlot(CONIPF.combined, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE) # 15 clusters

DimPlot(CONIPF.combined, reduction = "umap", group.by = "orig.ident")

DimPlot(CONIPF.combined, reduction = "umap", split.by = "orig.ident")

Identify which cluster has the enriched amount of certain marker genes

DotPlot(CONIPF.combined, features = c("EPCAM", "SFTPC", "AGER", "SCGB1A1")) + RotatedAxis()

“EPCAM”, “SFTPC”, “AGER”, “SCGB1A1” –> Epithelial cell –> clusters 5, 9

DotPlot(CONIPF.combined, features = c("CLDN5", "CCL21", "PECAM1", "EMCN")) + RotatedAxis()

“CLDN5”, “CCL21”, “PECAM1”, “EMCN” –> Endothelial cell –> clusters 1, 12

DotPlot(CONIPF.combined, features = c("COL1A2", "LUM", "PDGFRA","PDGFRB")) + RotatedAxis()

“COL1A2”, “LUM”, “PDGFRA”,“PDGFRB” –> Mesenchymal cell –> clusters 4, 6, 14

DotPlot(CONIPF.combined, features = c("PTPRC", "CD52", "AIF1", "TRBC2")) + RotatedAxis()

“PTPRC”, “CD52”, “AIF1”, “TRBC2” –> Immune cell –> clusters 0, 2, 3, 7, 8, 10, 11, 13

Rename cluster number with cell type name

CONIPF.combined <- RenameIdents(CONIPF.combined, 
                                `5` = "EPI", `9` = "EPI", `1` = "ENDO",`12` = "ENDO", 
                                `4` = "MESN", `6` = "MESN", `14` = "MESN", 
                                `0` = "IM", `2` = "IM", `3` = "IM", `7` = "IM",
                                `8` = "IM",`10` = "IM",`11` = "IM",`13` = "IM")

DimPlot(CONIPF.combined, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE)

FeaturePlot()

Visualize the location of these marker genes in UMAP plot

DefaultAssay(CONIPF.combined) <- "RNA"

FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features=c("EPCAM", "SFTPC", "AGER", "SCGB1A1"),
            label=TRUE, cols = c('lightgrey', 'blue'))

FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features=c("CLDN5", "CCL21", "PECAM1", "EMCN"),
            label=TRUE, cols = c('lightgrey', 'blue'))

FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features=c("COL1A2", "LUM", "PDGFRA","PDGFRB"),
            label=TRUE, cols = c('lightgrey', 'blue'))

FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features=c("PTPRC", "CD52", "AIF1", "TRBC2"),
            label=TRUE, cols = c('lightgrey', 'blue'))

Compare CTHRC1 gene expression in control vs disease

FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features = "CTHRC1", split.by = "orig.ident")

Adjust the contrast in the plot

P1 <- FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features = "LUM") # expression level 0-5 is shown
P2 <- FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features ="LUM", min.cutoff =1) # so that only expression level 1-5 is shown
P1|P2 # plots 1 and 2 side by side

Visualize co-expression of two features (genes)

FeaturePlot(CONIPF.combined, features = c("DCN", "LUM"), blend = TRUE)

Violin Plot

Visualize marker genes are highly expressed in each cell type

VlnPlot(CONIPF.combined, features = c("EPCAM", "CLDN5", "COL1A2", "PTPRC"), ncol = 2, pt.size = 0)

COL1A1 and CTHRC1 gene are upregulated in disease

VlnPlot(CONIPF.combined, features = c("COL1A1", "CTHRC1"), split.by = "orig.ident") # left is control, right is disease

DoHeatmap

DefaultAssay(CONIPF.combined) <- "RNA"
CONIPF.combined <- JoinLayers(CONIPF.combined, assay = "RNA") # FindAllMarkers() required JoinLayers
CONIPF.combined.markers <- FindAllMarkers(CONIPF.combined, only.pos = TRUE, min.pct = 0.25, logfc.threshold = 0.25)

# find top 5 genes for each cell type
CONIPF.combined.markers %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  top_n(n = 5, wt = avg_log2FC) -> top5

DefaultAssay(CONIPF.combined) <- "integrated"
DoHeatmap(CONIPF.combined, features = top5$gene)

Save Seurat Object as RDS file

It’s recommended to save the data as RDS file so that later on we can easily load the RDS file to R. RDS file eliminates the need to save data in the environment which can be memory heavy.

# To save as RDS: 
saveRDS(CONIPF.combined, file="CONIPF.combined.RDS")
# To read RDS into R:
CONIPF.combined <- readRDS("CONIPF.combined.RDS")
